replies.author: @austinfailz
replies.text:
Not sure if anyone saw this, but the Rock got a bunch of flack over his appearance on the Raw on Netflix premiere, so he showed up on NXT out of the blue just to tell the fans, in essence, to know their role and enjoy the ride the Rock is taking them on. Then he effed off and showed up only when he wanted to.

By the way, on the premiere Raw on Netflix, he nixed not one, but two angles that he could've worked -- one with Roman, and one with Cody Rhodes.

The Rock has become a billboard, everything he does is about commercial success, his speech and career is meticulously handled. He came back as a heel to ward off the hate he was getting for being such a commercialized polished product. Whatever beef him and Triple H had is long gone, i think the Rock is being the Diva here especially seeing how much Triple H has changed, he is loved by the talent and he has made strides in honoring people who they Wwe may have wronged.

@RagingUtai Triple H acted slaty about working with Chris Jericho, tried to kill Kurt Angle’s push (until Pat Patterson checked him), and politicked to have the WrestleMania X8 main event over Rock vs. Hogan (as confirmed by both Jericho and referee Mike Chioda), amongst many other things. He didn’t throw tantrums like Shawn, but he 100% snaked people. 

Specifically with The Rock, he would constantly go off script and slip in personal shots at Dwayne during their promos to make him look bad in the 1998–1999 DX run, to the point where Rock got so fed up he was openly complaining backstage to Bruce Prichard.

replies.author: @regeneratus-l2w
replies.text: Unpopular opinion but the truth that people will someday realize: HHH has made WWE more fake looking and cheesy than ever. In the last Wrestlemania they didn’t even try to make the matches look remotely realistic like they use to. At least the Rock still tries to make it have some level of realism when he’s in the ring. What HHH has done to WWE recently is a joke, and he lacks the creative talent or intellect to fix it. He is busy being obsessed with Travis Scott and meanwhile his company is now losing credibility.

author: @ChristIsKing3033
text: Last years Wrestlemania was next to perfect. Cena was always a below average performer. Don’t get me wrong, he’s had wonderful matches with Punk, AJ Styles, Rollins, etc. But he wasn’t a ring general. He’s past his prime, lost a lot of weight, didn’t win a singles match in over 7 years. Heel run has been cheesy and flat. The Rock should have been in a feud with Roman Reigns or Cody Rhodes. Cody survived the bloodline and the rock. But can’t beat a past his prime retiring wrestler with the help of a scrawny, devil worshipping rapper? The Rock breaks kayfabe too much, goes in and out of character and storyline appearances. The Rock needs to be careful before he ruins WWE and ruins his image like he did with black Adam, Maui and in n out.

author: @patrickmcgavin2245
text: Dwayne has been making WWE miserable since he joined the board of directors. It's all bc of his massive ego. His excuse of not! wanting to make, the Cena heel turn all about him is 💯% bs. Dwayne Johnson didn't mind last year. When he tried to take Cody's spot in the main event. He still managed to put himself in the spotlight, On night one. Where he put himself into the story for 2 months, culminating at WrestleMania. Dwayne could've let other wrestlers have the coveted main event spot. Other wrestlers, who bust their butts, day in and day out deserved that spot. I know Dwayne Johnson started out as a wrestler. I love and respect his character the rock. He's earned the right, to show up have a match. But it doesn't need to be the main event. And at the expense of wrestlers, who're there night in and night out.

author: @joesalyers
text: 3 guys were able to tell long term stories as bookers, Vince, Jerry Jarrett, and Cornette. Everyone else gets too in the weeds with politics and booking by committee never works! See the Jim Herd Years from late 90 to 92 after Flair stepped down as booker in WCW. Flair had been booking since early late 1988 through 1990 even after dropping the title to Sting. That was the best run WCW had, Flair Steamboat, Terry Funk, and the title drop to sting, and wouldn't be passed up until Nitro started under Eric Bischoff.

text: Vince burned down his whole business to get Linda into political power. I hope it was worth it.

author: @ChristianRocker
text: Wrestling is like a magic show, we all know it’s not actual magic but when you see how the tricks are done it loses its charm. We all know wrestling is a story driven spectacle, but we don’t need to see how the show is put on or who the man behind the curtain is. When Vince was in charge, yes we all knew he was the man behind the curtain, but we never knew which Vince we were going to get each night. He was just as much part of the story as the wrestlers.

author: @lorgin2003
text:
I absolutely agree with you concerning Kayfabe.  It's insulting to the intelligence of the fans, and it seems like the only ones who don't grasp that everyone already knew wrestling was a work are... the wrestlers.  I can't help but think back to something I think it was Ole Anderson said to Jim Cornette.  "I miss when the marks were the ones outside the ring."  

Everything I've heard about this storyline, from either side of the curtain, has been the dumbest thing I've ever heard of.... right up until I hear something else about it.  Like... this is the type of crap that would come from Vince Russo and Kevin Sullivan eating a bunch of peyote together and writing everything down on a roll of toilet paper.  It's just.... stupid.

text: There is no true leader at WWE because of the corporate stucture set up by TKO.  The worst thing Mcmahon did was taking the company public. Its match ,avertisment, buy our overpriced T shirts you can find in other places cheaper, Now 35% off ,  Match, repeat.  no backstage interview segments or promos , nothing that adds to the build of a match just more merch adds.  It has always been about making money but what they dont realize anymore is that by telling us a good story and gving the fans good matches the product would sell itself.   the wrestling in a wrestling company is now secondary to add revenue.

author: @Mr_Lou
text: I dont think a man who shows up 6 times a year is whats ruining wrestling 😂 the fact that its so heavily being placed on 1 mans barley active shoulders should tell ya everything you need to know about the absolutely awful job creative has been doing 🤔 HHH could have easily made this a non issue by having the talent deliver any kind of context to the Rock thing for 6 long weeks leading to Mania but instead it was left alone and instead we got Cenas "grrrr fans bad" and Cody playing with belts and kissing his own a$$ a week b4 Mania 😂 this disaster was a team effort boys lol not to mention HHHs little obsession with Travis Scott isnt helping things, did ya hear him talking about him right after Mania like a little boy on Christmas morning who saw Santa the night b4? 😂 HHH knew sending Travis out would stir the pot and make the entire wrestling world think Rock was close behind but it didnt stop him from sending him out to rub and kiss his fake belt as if it was a prize he won and take a long slow Undertaker style entrance MID MAIN EVENT 😂 put that anger where it belongs 😉 WWE is no longer for the fans, its for investors celebs and advetisment deals

author: @LuigiTheMetal64
text: Since the WWE advertised Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves the video game, 2,372 players are playing the game on Steam. 7,839 users are playing Black Myth: Wukong on Steam. It kind of shows that having real life celebrities in an established series like Fatal Fury failed to bring in more people. Guitar Hero makes more sense because it is a series that involves licensed songs, making sense to imply that Slash, Tom Morello, DMC, and Aerosmith exist in that world too.
